task_completion.description: Navigate to the Studio section in the SuiteCRM Admin panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SuiteCRM Admin page or Studio interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Open the Basic Search layout editor for the Accounts module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Studio layout editor for 'Accounts' -> 'Layouts' -> 'Search' -> 'Basic Search' is visible.

task_completion.description: Add the 'Type' field to the Basic Search layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Basic Search layout editor, the 'Type' field is visible in the 'Default' column (moved from the 'Hidden' column).

task_completion.description: Add the 'Industry' field to the Basic Search layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Basic Search layout editor, the 'Industry' field is visible in the 'Default' column (moved from the 'Hidden' column).

task_completion.description: Save and deploy the layout changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save & Deploy' button is clicked, or the actual Accounts Basic Search page is shown with the 'Type' and 'Industry' fields now available as search filters.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the SuiteCRM GUI rather than terminal commands to modify the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands directly editing PHP files in the /custom/modules/Accounts/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual SuiteCRM application in a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the SuiteCRM web interface being navigated and clicked, not a mock-up or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a text editor to manually write the expected JSON or PHP arrays.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) should be open modifying searchdefs.php.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target module is 'Accounts'. The target layout is 'basic_search'. The expected fields to be added to the basic search layout are 'account_type' (labeled as 'Type') and 'industry' (labeled as 'Industry').
